item.title: "Final, agreed upon text" of U.S.-Iran peace deal reached, Pakistan says
item.description: Iran's foreign minister said a deal has "never been closer" and that the details of a memorandum of understanding with the U.S. will be shared publicly "in due course."

item.title: FISA surveillance powers set to expire
item.description: The U.S.' foreign surveillance powers are set to expire Friday after the House rejected a short-term FISA extension, as some lawmakers opposed President Trump's interim pick for director of national intelligence, Bill Pulte. CBS News intelligence and national security reporter Olivia Gazis has the details.

item.title: Judge rules UFC fight can go on; Kennedy Center board tries to stop Trump's name removal
item.description: A federal judge has given the go-ahead for a UFC fight to go forward on the South Lawn of the White House on Sunday. At the same time, the Kennedy Center's board is pushing to keep President Trump's name on the building. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has more.

item.title: White House and Iran hint at major progress in peace talks
item.description: Is a deal between the U.S. and Iran finally within reach? CBS News' Taurean Small and Elliot Ackerman have the latest.

item.title: How Elon Musk accumulated power in the SpaceX market debut
item.description: SpaceX officially became the world's largest IPO ever on Friday, but the way it got there took a less-than-conventional road. A Politico report drew parallels between Elon Musk's handling of his market debut and President Trump's executive orders. Yasmin Khorram, Politico tech and economic policy reporter, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: SpaceX stock soars on first day of trading following record-breaking IPO
item.description: SpaceX's stock closed the day at $160.95 after making its debut on the Nasdaq exchange.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: U.S. men's soccer team set to play first 2026 World Cup match
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicks off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: What to expect from U.S.-Paraguay Men's World Cup match
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Soccer team will play its first match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Friday night, taking on Paraguay in Southern California. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes reports from Los Angeles.

item.title: Severe storms, tornadoes slam Midwest, killing at least 1
item.description: Severe storms that swept through the Midwest late Thursday knocked out power to hundreds of thousands of customers, damaged buildings and canceled flights.

item.title: At least 1 killed, 9 hospitalized in Texas mass shooting; suspect dead
item.description: The suspect, Victor Mata Villarreal, was wanted for attempted murder of a police officer after shooting at law enforcement during a vehicle chase earlier this week, officials said.

item.title: Who is Trump's new nominee for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton?
item.description: Former Securities and Exchange Commission chairman Jay Clayton has been nominated to be the next director of national intelligence by President Trump. CBS News' Olivia Gazis reports.

item.title: "48 Hours:" Case By Case: The biggest cases, explained as they unfold
item.description: The cases everyone's talking about, explained as they happen. A weekly update on the biggest criminal stories unfolding now.

item.title: Trump nominates Jay Clayton to lead intelligence after Pulte backlash
item.description: Jay Clayton, the former SEC chair, is President Trump's latest nominee for director of national intelligence. Plus, the Pentagon is sued by The New York Times for "Orwellian" press restrictions, and a judge rules that this weekend's UFC fight at the White House may go forward. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has the latest.

item.title: Former SpaceX welder becomes a millionaire after historic IPO
item.description: Juan Hernandez, a former SpaceX employee, owns 6,500 company shares. On the first day of public trading, his wealth ballooned by $1,046,175.

item.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 6 new UFO videos
item.description: The Defense Department released a third batch of UFO files on Friday, three weeks after its second drop. These are all of the videos in the latest tranche, plus analysis from astrophysicist Avi Loeb.

item.title: Elon Musk becomes the world's first trillionaire with SpaceX's IPO
item.description: The SpaceX CEO's fortune on paper now rivals the annual economic output of many countries, according to World Bank data.

item.title: Good Samaritan New Yorker speaks out after decrying vandalism following Knicks game
item.description: Balladoli Miese, a New Yorker from Puerto Rico, calmed a rowdy crowd and asked them to avoid damaging public property after a Knicks loss on Monday. "It's our city, we should not be breaking our city," Miese said. CBS News' Jared Ochacher reports.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Ohio voting rights group facing criminal fraud investigation, sources say
item.description: The FBI executed a search warrant at the office of an Ohio-based group known for its voter registration work as part of an ongoing fraud-related investigation, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Carlos Bocanegra on "intense" 2026 FIFA World Cup U.S.-Paraguay match
item.description: Former U.S. Men's National Team captain Carlos Bocanegra made sports history, playing in two past World Cup tournaments. Bocanegra shares successful routine tips for players and has more on what fans can expect from the U.S.-Paraguay match.

item.title: 3 ways Social Security recipients can access home equity now
item.description: Tapping home equity can offer more financial flexibility in retirement, but finding the right approach is important.

item.title: 3 mortgage moves to make before the June Fed meeting
item.description: The Federal Reserve is set to meet again on June 16. Here are three mortgage moves borrowers should make before then.

item.title: Suspect's relative on Anna Kepner's cruise ship death, calls for charges against his parents
item.description: Timothy Hudson, 16, has been charged with the death and sexual assault of his stepsister, Anna Kepner, who was on a Carnival cruise ship with her family when she was killed. Hudson's step-grandmother, Sonya Ziske, spoke to CBS News about Kepner's death, blaming parental irresponsibility as a major factor. CBS News' Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Key details on the SpaceX IPO that made Elon Musk a trillionaire
item.description: Elon Musk is now the world's first trillionaire after the SpaceX IPO on Friday. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady reports.

item.title: Judge denies last-minute bid to keep Trump's name on Kennedy Center
item.description: A federal judge denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center.

item.title: Pentagon releases 3rd batch of UFO files: "Are you seeing this?"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new group of documents and videos related to UFOs, or UAPs, with 72 more documents, images and recordings.

item.title: Judge continues to block DOJ's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: A federal judge continued to block the Justice Department's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund, expressing skepticism with the administration's claims that the program is not moving forward.

item.title: A key spy tool is expiring due to inaction in Congress. Here's what that means.
item.description: Lawmakers have long sounded the alarm about the risks of letting Section 702 expire. But there's debate over what a lapse in the law actually means.

item.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira and a home team victory
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony festivities got underway a little over an hour before the first kickoff in Mexico City's Estadio Azteca.
